name: Eekso — The Art Room (Blind Fight)
dmNotes:
SET-PIECE 2 — the blind fight. The gimmick is that DARKVISION DOESN'T HELP. The room isn't dark; it's FULL. Thousands of hanging bones and trinkets on threads block line of sight physically, so every PC is fighting a heavily obscured battlefield while the dead things in here don't need eyes at all. Rules are in 'How It Works' below — read that before you run it.

The pawn upstairs told them none of it is real and they can walk right through. That lie is why they'll be deep in the gallery before the first thing touches them.

STAT BLOCK STILL OWED (Wes asked): custom zombie-things. Until then, SRD works fine — see the roster note. Keep the reserve hidden and only spend it if they chew through the first wave.

FIRE IS THE INTENDED OUT. If a player burns the strings, reward it hard — the gallery goes up, the room lights, and the fight changes completely. Let them be clever.

ambience.file: ambience/Sounds Dogs Love - Wood Chimes - Videos For Dogs.mp3

scriptText:
# The Art Room

The doorway opens into a room you cannot see the far wall of, because the room is full.

Threads hang from the ceiling. Thousands of them — cords, wires, gut, braided hair — and from every one hangs something small. A finger bone. A ring. A milk tooth. A button. A coin, a buckle, a lock of hair tied with ribbon, a child's carved animal, a knucklebone, a knucklebone, a knucklebone.

They hang at every height, from the ceiling to your knees, in rows and drifts and curtains, on and on into the dark past where your light reaches.

And they *move*. The air in here shifts, and ten thousand small hard things turn on their strings and touch each other, and the whole room makes a sound like rain on dry leaves that never quite stops.

> [!dm] Take your time here. This is the most striking room in the dungeon and the description is the encounter's first half.
>
> The party has just been told **"none of it is real, you can walk right through."**
>
> - Every object was taken off a person. **Investigation DC 12** — these are keepsakes, not decorations. Somebody chose each one.
> - It's arranged. There's *composition* here: sight-lines, spacing, height. Someone stands in this room and enjoys it.
> - **Perception DC 15** while moving through: some of the threads have been cut recently and re-tied at different heights. It's been *rearranged*, and not long ago.

## How It Works

> [!dm] **Read this before running the fight.**
>
> **The room is heavily obscured — by objects, not darkness.** Darkvision does nothing. A light spell does nothing. You cannot see through ten thousand hanging bones.
>
> - Every creature is effectively **blinded** with regard to anything more than 5 ft away
> - **Attacking something you can't see:** disadvantage, and you must guess its space — wrong guess is an automatic miss
> - **Being attacked by something you can't see:** the attacker has advantage
> - **Movement** through the gallery is **difficult terrain** — you're pushing through curtains of hanging line
> - **Noise gives you away:** any creature that moves more than 5 ft on its turn is *heard*. Its space is known to everything in the room until its next turn.
> - **Finding something by ear:** as an action, Perception vs. the creature's Stealth. Success pinpoints it until it moves.
>
> **The dead things in here do not use eyes.** Give them blindsight 60 ft. They hunt normally. The party is fighting blind against things that aren't — which is the entire point of the room, and it should feel genuinely unfair for the first two rounds.

## The Fight

{{music:stalk}} Somewhere off to the left, the sound changes. A patch of the rain-on-leaves noise goes hard and fast — a whole curtain of bone swinging at once — and then stops.

Then the same thing behind you.

Then close.

{{music:fight}}

> [!dm] **Opening move:** don't show them a monster. Show them *disturbance*. Let them hear it cross the room, and let one of them get hit by something they never saw.
>
> **Roster (SRD until the custom block exists):**
> - **6 Zombies** — slow, relentless, Undead Fortitude makes them stay up
> - **3 Wights** — the real threat; they should be the ones flanking out of the dark
> - Give every one of them **blindsight 60 ft**
> - That's ~2,400 XP against 4x lvl 6 + Cumb — a low-moderate budget on paper, which the blindness turns into a real fight
>
> **Hidden reserve** (only if they're carving through it): 2 more wights waiting in the far section. Bring them in as *new sounds from a direction that was quiet*.
>
> **Running it well:**
> - Describe positions by SOUND. "Somewhere ahead and to your right, low."
> - Make them declare which square they're swinging at
> - When a PC gets grabbed, the thing that has them is silent — no growl, no breath. Just weight.

## Burning It

{{music:burn}} {{amb:nox-fire-big|vol=40%|in=2s}}

> [!dm] **The intended clever answer.** The threads are gut, hair, cord and old dry cloth. They take fire *instantly*.
>
> If a player torches the gallery, give it to them fully:
> - Fire runs along the strings in every direction at once — the room lights up like a net of sparks
> - Within a round the hanging objects start falling. The obscurement is **gone**. Everyone can see.
> - Now the party can see what they're fighting, and also see the whole room, and it is worse than not seeing it
> - Burning debris: anything in the open takes a small hit (1d6 fire) at the end of its turn for 2–3 rounds
>
> Trade one problem for another. That's a good deal and they earned it.
>
> Don't hint at this. Let a player find it.

## After

{{music:after}}

When it's done, the room is very quiet.

If it burned, there's a floor of ash and small blackened things and a smell that will not come out of their clothes. If it didn't, the strings are still turning, and the ones the fight tore down lie in drifts underfoot.

> [!dm] Let somebody pick one up. That's the beat.
>
> - A wedding ring, still sized for the finger it came off
> - Two teeth on one thread — a child's, and an adult's, tied together
> - Something with a Koviz maker's mark, if you want to reach for a knife
>
> **Do not put treasure here.** The room's payoff is the moment a player realises how many people this is.
>
> Past the far side: the next stair down. Switch to **'Eekso — The Ritual'**.

ideas.text: The hangings are ARRANGED — sight-lines and spacing. Somebody stands here and enjoys it.
Some threads were cut and re-tied recently. The room has been rearranged, and not long ago.
Describe every enemy position by sound only; make players name the square they swing at
The thing that grabs a PC makes no sound at all — no growl, no breath, just weight
Burning the gallery removes the obscurement — and then they can see the whole room, which is worse
Two teeth tied to one thread — a child's and an adult's

entities.name: The hanging gallery
entities.note: Heavily obscured by OBJECTS — darkvision and light spells do nothing. Difficult terrain. Movement makes noise.

entities.name: The dead in the gallery ⚑ custom block owed
entities.note: SRD stand-in: 6 zombies + 3 wights, all with blindsight 60 ft. ~2,400 XP.

entities.name: Hidden reserve (2 wights)
entities.note: Only if the party carves through the first wave. Arrive as new sound from a quiet direction.

entities.name: The keepsakes
entities.note: Every object came off a person. Investigation DC 12. No treasure here — the payoff is the count.
